group.title: Indigenous Youth Leadership Programme Scholars: Digital Storytelling project

results.title: Stories and songs of the people: the second stage
results.creator_credit: Johnnie Aseron, Fiona Cochrane, Film Victoria and F-Reel Pty Ltd
results.brief_description: Participants in the Stories and Songs of the People weekend tell their personal stories and provide a perspective on living in a world dominated by white culture. The struggles of indigenous peoples in Australia and some other parts of the world are explored through these individual experiences. A selection of music and songs performed during the weekend provide a backdrop to the discussion.

results.title: Vicki's story
results.creator_credit: Vicki Atkinson and ACMI Digital Storytelling
results.brief_description: Vicki reminisces about her family and childhood and how she looked up to her parents as role models hoping that what she has learned from them can be passed on to her own two daughters. This is her message.
results.description: Vicki reminisces about her family and childhood and how she looked up to her parents as role models hoping that what she has learned from them can be passed on to her own two daughters. This is her message.

results.title: A Place called Gapuwiyak
results.creator_credit: Nicole La Gerche and ACMI Digital Storytelling
results.brief_description: This is Nicole’s story of her experiences living in East Arnhem, of being adopted into and accepted by the Wunungmurra family and the Yolngu community.

results.creator_credit: Marianne Atkinson and ACMI Digital Storytelling

results.title: ASHE: the way of life
results.creator_credit: Colin Colger and ACMI Digital Storytelling
results.brief_description: After the death of his mother and losing his way to a life of crime, Colin, a sports loving Shepparton boy, was offered a new beginning - a lifeline - through ASHE (Academy of Health Sports and Education).

results.title: Baby girl
results.creator_credit: Chrissy Ponting and ACMI Digital Storytelling
results.brief_description: Life is very challenging for Chrissy as a teenage mum but through her love for daughter Lakeisha combined with the support of family and friends, she has been able to complete her school studies and begin training as a nurse. This is dedicated to her baby girl.
results.description: Life is very challenging for Chrissy as a teenage mum but through her love for daughter Lakeisha combined with the support of family and friends, she has been able to complete her school studies and begin training as a nurse. This is dedicated to her baby girl.

results.title: To my dearest beloved one - Shewitey Mearey
results.brief_description: A personal account created by a participant in the ACMI Digital Storytelling Workshop “Journeys with Breast Cancer” Created in association with Breast Cancer Network Australia. “I said to the doctor, I don’t care if I die tomorrow but I don’t want to leave my daughter alone. He said to me that first I had to live before I could have another child.”
results.title_for_label: To my dearest beloved one - Shewitey Mearey

results.title: Fair treatment
results.creator_credit: Tim Johnson, Chris McGill and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: This program examines some of the most important issues facing people living with HIV and contains interviews with people who are HIV positive. It focuses on the availability of drugs for HIV patients, and the lack of action and apathy shown by Australian governments. It also explores the outlook on life of the interviewees, how the stigma of AIDS, the death of friends and loved ones has affected them and finally examines their attitudes to death and dying.
